## Parallel Execution Waves

### Wave 1: Setup (Read Sources)
**Agent**: senior-backend-engineer
**Tasks**: T001 (serial), then T002-T006 (parallel — different files)
**Quality Gate**: All source materials read, command details extracted

### Wave 2: Prompt Spec Content (US4)
**Agent**: senior-backend-engineer
**Tasks**: T007 → T008 → T009 → T010 → T011 (serial, same file)
**Parallel slot**: T012 can run alongside T010-T011 (independent factual corrections)
**Then**: T013 (OpenClaw extension, depends on T007-T009)
**Quality Gate**: Prompt spec content complete, all 4 commands covered

### Wave 3: Prompt Spec Rename (US4)
**Agent**: senior-backend-engineer
**Tasks**: T014 → T015 (serial — rename then update references)
**Quality Gate**: Prompt spec renamed, no broken references

### Wave 4: Developer Guide Core (US1+US2)
**Agent**: senior-backend-engineer
**Tasks**: T016 → T017 → T018 → T019 (serial, same file, section order matters)
**Quality Gate**: All 4 command sections in guide with interpretation

### Wave 5: Quick Start + OpenClaw + Appendices (US3+US5+US6)
**Agent**: senior-backend-engineer
**Tasks**: T020, T021 → T022 → T023, T024, T025, T026 (serial for safety, same file)
**Quality Gate**: All content additions complete

### Wave 6: Validation
**Agent**: code-reviewer
**Tasks**: T027, T028 + T029 (parallel), T030, T031 (must be last)
**Quality Gate**: All validation checks pass

## Time Estimates

| Wave | Estimated Duration | Notes |
|------|-------------------|-------|
| Wave 1 | 15 min | Mostly reading, high parallelism |
| Wave 2 | 60-90 min | Substantial writing, serial same-file |
| Wave 3 | 10 min | Git rename + reference search |
| Wave 4 | 60-90 min | 4 large section insertions |
| Wave 5 | 40-55 min | Mixed: callout, 3 OpenClaw steps, appendices, glossary |
| Wave 6 | 20-30 min | Cross-checking, consistency review |
| **Total** | **3.5-5 hours** (serial) / **2-3 hours** (with parallelism) | |

## Notes

- **Single-file constraint**: Most tasks edit the same 2 files. True parallelism is limited to cross-file work (Wave 1 reads, Wave 6 checks). Same-file tasks are executed serially regardless of [P] markers.
- **MVP delivery**: Completing Waves 1-4 (19 tasks) delivers the P0 success criteria — all 4 commands documented with interpretation.
